bug-20260529-physics-tick-systems-no-op-stub-demo-frozen M1/M2: the 2D and 3D rapier backends are intentionally mirror-shaped per constraint C-3 (dual-impl symmetry). ensureBody (ECS->Rapier desc construction) and the three-phase tick systems (physicsSyncBackend/physicsStepSimulation/physicsWriteback) are structurally identical across both packages but bind distinct Rapier WASM modules (@dimforge/rapier2d-compat vs rapier3d-compat) with dimension-specific APIs (Vec2 {x,y} + scalar rotation vs Vec3 {x,y,z} + Quat; cuboid 2-arg vs 3-arg half-extents; no z-axis in 2D). Extracting a shared abstract base would require generics over the two incompatible Rapier module types + type-level dispatch on dimension, adding complexity beyond the cost of two dimension-adapted implementations. Same mirror-shaped-by-design idiom as the existing packages/gltf/src/parse-animation.ts <-> parse-skin.ts whitelist entry above. ImplementReviewer round-1 mechanicalSelfChecks documented this duplication as deliberately kept (C-3).

bug-20260529-physics-tick-systems-no-op-stub-demo-frozen M2 (plan-strategy D-1): the rapier physics tick systems deliberately replicate the advanceAnimationPlayer ECS-system registration idiom -- closure over `world`, empty query tuple, archetype-graph manual traversal (graph.byId loop + componentIds.includes guards + column-view extraction + per-row entity re-encode). This is the engine's already-proven system-authoring pattern; D-1 explicitly chose to copy it rather than invent a new one, so the archetype-traversal skeleton is byte-similar by design. Extracting a shared `_archetype-walk` helper would couple the physics backend package to runtime internals it intentionally accesses only through the thin InternalWorldSurface shim. Charter F1: each system file reads top-to-bottom as a self-contained tick recipe.
